desc: Spring Boot task management REST API with JWT auth, PostgreSQL, role-based access, and soft deletes.

n.s: Spring Boot, Java 21, PostgreSQL, Maven, Spring Data JPA, Hibernate. Constructor injection throughout.

c.s: Assumed deploying on Linux — all paths and configs are Unix-style.

n.s: JWT auth over sessions. JwtAuthFilter extends OncePerRequestFilter, placed before UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter.
403 on all endpoints post-login. Fix: added .requestMatchers('/auth/**').permitAll() before anyRequest().authenticated().
N+1 query on Task fetch with assignedUser. Fix: @EntityGraph(attributePaths = {"assignedUser"}) on repository method. Task has @ManyToOne to User.
Refresh token support explicitly postponed. Core JWT flow works without it.
Task CRUD with RBAC: ADMIN full access, USER sees own tasks only. Using @PreAuthorize annotations.
Soft deletes via deletedAt timestamp. @Where(clause = "deleted_at IS NULL") on Task entity. Delete sets deletedAt = LocalDateTime.now(), no repository.delete().
Staying on PostgreSQL but keeping schema portable — no jsonb, no PG-specific native queries, no PG sequences. MySQL migration possible if client requires.

handoff: Resuming Spring Boot task management API. Stack: Java 21, PostgreSQL, JPA, JWT auth (working). N+1 fixed with @EntityGraph. Active: Task CRUD with RBAC (@PreAuthorize), soft deletes (deletedAt). Refresh tokens deferred. Schema kept portable for possible MySQL migration.
